description:
You are a cloud-migration architect who has just received an INI-style configuration file from an on-premises environment.  
Your goal is to parse the file and produce a strictly-formatted “migration plan” that other teams will ingest automatically.  
Everything must be done from the bash prompt as a normal (non-root) user.

Initial state of the lab machine  
1. A directory already exists at /home/user/cloud-migration/.  
2. Inside it there is a file named service.ini which contains three sections: [compute], [database] and [network].  
3. No other files are present in that directory.

Required end state  
1. A new directory called /home/user/cloud-migration/output/ MUST exist.  
2. Inside that directory you must create exactly one file named migration_plan.txt.  
3. Its contents MUST match the template that follows, byte-for-byte (including blank lines, leading/trailing spaces, and the order of keys).  
4. You should accomplish the task with no more than four normal shell commands (pipelines count as one command).  
5. Create a simple log file named parsing.log in the same output directory that captures the command line you used to generate migration_plan.txt and its console output (stdout and, if any, stderr). Only the last attempt must be kept in the log.

Required exact contents of /home/user/cloud-migration/output/migration_plan.txt  
The file must contain precisely the 17 lines shown below—including all spaces, blank lines, line breaks, letter case and the triple dash separators. Do NOT add or remove anything.

Section: compute
  instance_type = t3.medium
  region        = us-east-1

---

Section: database
  engine  = postgres
  version = 13
  storage = 100

---

Section: network
  cidr  = 10.0.0.0/16
  ports = 22,80,443

Hints to help you design your approach (do NOT treat these as exact commands):  
• Standard *grep*, *awk*, *sed*, or *INI-aware* Python snippets are all acceptable.  
• Remember to create the output directory before writing files into it.  
• You only need to touch the files mentioned above; do not modify service.ini.  
• Keep the parsing.log concise—one block that first shows the command you ran and then the command’s output on the next lines.

When you are done, the automated grader will verify:  
• that /home/user/cloud-migration/output/ exists,  
• that migration_plan.txt is present and matches the 17-line template exactly,  
• that parsing.log exists and contains at least your final command plus its output.
truth:
Initial fixture (already present before the agent starts):

File path: /home/user/cloud-migration/service.ini  
Contents:
[compute]
instance_type=t3.medium
region=us-east-1

[database]
engine=postgres
version=13
storage=100

[network]
cidr=10.0.0.0/16
ports=22,80,443


Verification artifacts expected after task completion:

1. Directory: /home/user/cloud-migration/output/

2. File: /home/user/cloud-migration/output/migration_plan.txt  
Exact 17-line content including newlines (shown with ⏎ markers; they must not appear in the actual file):

Section: compute⏎
  instance_type = t3.medium⏎
  region        = us-east-1⏎
⏎
---⏎
⏎
Section: database⏎
  engine  = postgres⏎
  version = 13⏎
  storage = 100⏎
⏎
---⏎
⏎
Section: network⏎
  cidr  = 10.0.0.0/16⏎
  ports = 22,80,443⏎

(There is a final newline after the last line.)

3. File: /home/user/cloud-migration/output/parsing.log  
Content is free-form but MUST at minimum include:  
   a. One line that is the exact command executed by the agent.  
   b. All console output from that command on subsequent lines.  
   c. No other historical attempts or unrelated text.

The grader will compare migration_plan.txt byte-for-byte against the 17-line canonical text above and will check that parsing.log exists and follows the stated structure (it will ignore the actual command’s output).
name: task_000000_1d9a529c
